chunk_summary: This section discusses the differential diagnosis of persistent nipple and breast pain in breastfeeding, referencing clinical protocols and guidelines for management, including mastitis and other causes.
learning_objectives: Identify common and uncommon causes of persistent nipple and breast pain during breastfeeding.
Understand the role of mastitis and its spectrum in breastfeeding-related pain.
Apply evidence-based guidelines for diagnosing and managing persistent pain in lactating individuals.
key_facts: Persistent nipple and breast pain can result from infections (e.g., mastitis), trauma, or dermatological conditions.
ABM Clinical Protocol #26 provides a structured approach to managing persistent breastfeeding pain.
Mastitis affects up to 10% of breastfeeding individuals and can range from mild inflammation to abscess formation.
The WHO guidelines emphasize early recognition and treatment of mastitis to prevent complications.
ACOG highlights the importance of addressing breastfeeding challenges to improve maternal and infant health outcomes.
reference_quote: ABM Clinical Protocol #26: Persistent Pain with Breastfeeding provides a comprehensive framework for evaluating and managing persistent nipple and breast pain.
